name: patent-search-mcp-server
description: MCP server for the AI Patent Search Generator — 11 tools for patent intelligence: dossier, prosecution, oa_analyze, search/query, similar, citations, family, examiner, cpc, balance.
long_description:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for the **AI Patent Search Generator** — patent dossiers, prosecution history, Office Action AI analysis, citation/family/CPC lookups, and Google Patents search.

Works in Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, ChatGPT-with-MCP, and any other MCP-compatible client.

## Tools (11)

- **dossier** — full patent intelligence: bibliographic, claims, citations, family, classifications, examiner stats. 3 credits; free on 24h cache hit.
- **prosecution** — USPTO file-wrapper documents (Office Actions, responses, amendments). Free.
- **oa_analyze** — AI analysis of USPTO Office Actions: rejection grounds, cited prior art, suggested response arguments. First 5/application free; 1 credit each thereafter.
- **search** — multi-strategy patent search against Google Patents (telescoping / onion-ring / faceted). 1 credit.
- **query** — generate a single optimized Boolean query for manual paste into Google Patents. 1 credit.
- **similar** — Google Patents similar-documents ranking. Free.
- **citations** — backward + forward citation graph, examiner-cited flagged. Free.
- **family** — patent family (continuations, divisionals, foreign counterparts). Free.
- **examiner** — examiner name, art unit, allowance rate, average pendency. Free.
- **cpc** — CPC classification code lookup. Free.
- **balance** — credit balance + subscription status. Free.

Requires an API key minted from the AI Patent Search Generator Chrome extension's Admin tab.

PATENT_SEARCH_API_KEY.title: Patent Search API Key
PATENT_SEARCH_API_KEY.description: API key minted from the AI Patent Search Generator Chrome extension's Admin tab. Format: psg_live_... or psg_test_...

PATENT_SEARCH_API_BASE.title: API Base URL (optional)
PATENT_SEARCH_API_BASE.description: Override the API base URL. Default: https://us-central1-solicitation-matcher-extension.cloudfunctions.net/ai/v1

tools.name: balance
tools.description: Return the current credit balance and subscription status for the authenticated AI Patent Search Generator account. Use this before calling paid tools (dossier, oa_analyze, query, search) to verify credits are available.

tools.name: dossier
tools.description: Fetch a comprehensive intelligence dossier for a patent by publication number. Returns bibliographic data, independent claims, backward + forward citations, patent family, CPC classifications, examiner info, and similar documents. Costs 3 credits on a fresh fetch; subsequent calls within 24 hours read from cache and are free. The headline tool - most patent-analysis workflows start here.

tools.name: prosecution
tools.description: Retrieve the USPTO file-wrapper documents (office actions, responses, amendments, IDS, notices) for a US patent or application. Free. Returns each documents ID, mail date, category, and short description - IDs can be passed to oa_analyze for AI analysis.

tools.name: oa_analyze
tools.description: AI-analyze a USPTO Office Action document to extract rejection grounds (102, 103, 112, etc.), cited prior art, and suggested response arguments. Two invocation forms: (1) pass only patentNumber and the server auto-picks the most recent Office Action; (2) pass both applicationNumber and documentId for explicit selection. First 5 analyses per application are free; subsequent analyses cost 1 credit each.

tools.name: examiner
tools.description: Get the assigned examiners name, art unit, total applications handled, allowance rate, and average pendency for a US patent. Free. Useful for prosecution strategy and risk assessment.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Generate a single optimized Boolean search query for Google Patents from a natural-language description of an invention or technology. Returns the Boolean string only - does NOT execute the search. Use search if you want ranked hits instead. Costs 1 credit.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Execute a patent search against Google Patents server-side and return ranked hits. Given a natural-language description and a search strategy, the server: (1) extracts technical concepts, (2) generates Boolean queries per the chosen strategy, (3) runs each query against Google Patents, (4) dedupes results by publication number. Strategies: telescoping (3 queries: broad/moderate/narrow - recommended default), onion-ring (layered queries adding one concept at a time), faceted (multiple two-concept pair queries). Costs 1 credit.

tools.name: similar
tools.description: Return Google Patents similar documents ranking for a given patent - a list of patents that Google considers technically related based on its own semantic models. Free. Distinct from search (which generates new queries from a description); this tool finds patents related to an existing one.

tools.name: citations
tools.description: Return backward and/or forward citations for a patent. Backward = patents this one cites (its prior art). Forward = patents that cite this one (downstream impact). Each citation includes whether it was examiner-cited (load-bearing on patentability) vs applicant-cited. Free. Lighter payload than dossier when you only need citation lists.

tools.name: family
tools.description: Return the patent family for a given patent - continuations, divisionals, and national counterparts (foreign equivalents). Useful for confirming worldwide IP coverage and identifying related applications still pending. Free.

tools.name: cpc
tools.description: Look up a Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) code and return its section, class, subclass, and group context. Accepts any level of code - section letter (e.g. H), class (H01), subclass (H01M), main group (H01M10/00), or subgroup (H01M10/0525). Free. v1.0 dataset covers all sections + ~80 common subclasses; subgroup-level descriptions land in v1.1.
